Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

This should not use 'Test-Parameters: trivial'

Patch Set 3:

So the story told is that trivial was meant for cases of build patches and when adding a new test. trivial was meant to limit the test to sanity-lnet.sh and sanity.sh. So when adding a new test with no lustre internal changes do we really need to run every single test? In the past I never used trivial and Andreas scolded me for wasting precious test resources. So what is the story now?

Patch Set 3:

In this case, it appears that the previous splice test caused failures on newer kernels that were not hit because "trivial" is only testing on RHEL7.x clients and servers.

Patch Set 3:

So trivial is no longer useful :-(

Patch Set 3:

PCC+splice+O_DIRECT is a corner case inside a corner case inside a corner case. Don't you think this is worth testing on more than just CentOS 7.8 clients before landing?

parents.subject: LU-15404 ldiskfs: truncate during setxattr leads to kernel panic

commit.subject: LU-13745 test: add PCC test for splice handling
commit.message:
LU-13745 test: add PCC test for splice handling

Add a test to exercise splice() and eventually sendfile() handling
with PCC.

Test-Parameters: trivial env=ONLY=21 testlist=sanity-pcc

Change-Id: Iae2517110c7332a81d0334232772ef1844d320ae
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
